---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-worldmap3.png')
background-size: 105%
background-class: top
class: middle

.center[.size50[**III. Why is it so Hard to Cooperate with Other Countries?**]]

<br>

.size50[
**Today's Agenda**

- Review outlines and write the paper
]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2024)
]]

???



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.pull-left[

<br>
<br>

.size70[.center[**For Today**]]

<br>
<br>

.size70[.center[**Draft an Outline**]]

]

.pull-right[

.size30[**Intro**
+ What is the question?
+ Why do we care?
+ Thesis statement
]

.size30[**Apply/Evaluate Theory 1**
+ Evidence x 2
]

.size30[**Apply/Evaluate Theory 2**
+ Evidence x 2
]

.size30[**Apply/Evaluate Theory 3**
+ Evidence x 2
]]


???


### Everybody ready to go with this?

#### - Outline in hand?

<br>

Ok, time to get some feedback!

Today you will get feedback from two people and have a chance to revise your outline to reflect that feedback.

In a moment I'll split you into pairs...




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_cubes_lighter3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size55[.center[**Review the Outlines in Pairs**]]

### Person 1: Present your Outline
.size35[
+ Explain the details and why you believe the evidence is high quality enough for the purpose
]

???

#### Person 1 presents their paper outline

--

### Person 2: To be convincing to you...
.size35[
+ Is EACH theory applied correctly?
+ Is EACH theory evaluated using the same criteria?
+ Is EACH piece of evidence high enough quality?
]

???

#### Person 2 gives feedback focused on helping person 1 make it more convincing

This criteria piece is new

- Two key elements to make this a coherent argument paper

1. A strong thesis statement that carries through the whole paper, and

2. Each theory should be evaluated using the same criteria
    - Need this to tie all the pieces together clearly.

### Questions on this?
 
<br>

*YOU:* Make sure to do this 2x!



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_cubes_lighter3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, inverse

.pull-left[

<br>

<br>

<br>

.size70[.center[**Revise your Outline**]]

]

.pull-right[

.size35[
**Intro**

**Apply/Evaluate Theory 1**

**Apply/Evaluate Theory 2**

**Apply/Evaluate Theory 3**

**Conclusion**
]]

???

Now that you've heard some more arguments and gotten feedback on yours it's time to revise.

- Reflect on the feedback, revise your outline and start writing!

- Tighten up those premises

<br>

### Why don't we need a counter-argument paragraph here?

- (Critical analysis through the whole thing!)

<br>

Paper is due next week!
